query: from_db_value convert database value to python object on a model field

CORRECTED (F7/loop1 gate): base Field in fields/__init__.py has NO from_db_value method; the real implementation is JSONField.from_db_value at json.py:87. Actual rank3 now matches. | status corrected 2026-07-03 gate run on c77beea: red since 0066b22 clean-slate removal, stale 'passing' never updated; same breakage independently documented by F7-f1b-wrong-expectation

query: Field deconstruct base class name path args kwargs migration
origin: eval-v2 N=1 audit F1 sub-bug: displayed score is the STALE pre-rerank hybrid score; results not ordered by it (rank1=0.780 < rank5=0.798). Ship a real, monotonic ranking score regardless of F1.1.
resolved: loop2 UPG-11.2: score is now the final post-rerank composite score; verified monotonic [1.2, 1.0124, 1.0051, 0.9978, 0.9905].

query: how to write a custom model field with deconstruct and from_db_value
origin: eval-v2 N=1 audit F2: 8792 indexed python/html/javascript/markdown ONLY. Django ships docs as .txt; docs/howto/custom-model-fields.txt (the single best resource for the task) was invisible to search. Opus D found it via Glob+Read. | status corrected 2026-07-03 gate run on c77beea: red since 0066b22 clean-slate removal, stale 'passing' never updated; same breakage independently documented by F18-doc-intent-not-boosted-enough
resolved: loop4 UPG-11.11: is_doc_intent_query() classifies 'how to ...' queries; on doc-intent, forced-inclusion suppressed AND doc-prose quality multiplier raised to 1.0 (from 0.70). docs/howto/custom-model-fields.txt surfaces in top-5. F1/F1b/F4/F5/F7/F8 code-intent queries unregressed (forced-inclusion still active, doc-prose still 0.70).

query: understand the base Field class lifecycle and validation
origin: eval-v2 N=1 audit F3: comprehension queries return a 2-5k chunk; model re-read the 12-14k fields/__init__.py 4-8x/session because search offers no expand-to-full-symbol/file affordance. Encode as metric once the affordance exists; currently a known gap.
resolved: loop2 UPG-11.4: symbol_start_line and symbol_end_line now present in /v1/search REST response. Verified: rank1 returns symbol_start_line=73 symbol_end_line=134.

query: Field deconstruct base class name path args kwargs migration
origin: loop1 UPG-11.1 gate: indexer stores symbol_name as bare leaf only (e.g. 'deconstruct', never 'Field.deconstruct'). The symbol_identity_boost qualified-match path (+0.20 for Class.method) is dead code at query time because re.split('[.:]+', symbol_name) always yields a single-element list. All deconstruct overrides get identical +0.10 leaf boost; discrimination is impossible. Fix: store symbol_name as 'Class.method' (using the class_context already tracked in _collect_chunks_ast at indexer.py:306) OR extract class prefix from content '# class: X' at query time in _apply_quality_and_dedup. | status corrected 2026-07-03 gate run on c77beea: red since 0066b22 clean-slate removal, stale 'passing' never updated
resolved: loop2 UPG-11.10: symbol_name now stored/displayed as 'Class.method'; UPG-11.7 forced-inclusion lifts Field.deconstruct to rank1.

query: Field deconstruct base class name path args kwargs migration
origin: loop1 UPG-11.1 gate: Field.deconstruct (lines 606-705, ~100 lines with docstring) does not appear in the top-50 hybrid candidate pool for the F1 query. The boost is applied post-retrieval and cannot rescue a chunk that never entered the pool. Root cause: the long docstring diverges in embedding space from the short concrete query; BM25 scores are also low because keyword overlap is diluted. Fix options: (1) index the method signature+first-line as a separate short chunk, (2) expand vector fetch pool beyond _RERANK_TOP_K_UNFILTERED=60, (3) store symbol metadata for forced inclusion of exact-name matches. | status corrected 2026-07-03 gate run on c77beea: red since 0066b22 clean-slate removal, stale 'passing' never updated
resolved: loop2 UPG-11.7: forced-inclusion unions all chunks whose symbol leaf matches a query token into the pool before rerank. Field.deconstruct now rank1.

query: list all database migrations for a project
origin: loop1 UPG-11.1 gate: symbol_identity_boost fires a +0.10 leaf boost for any symbol whose leaf is a common English word that appears in the query. Examples: leaf='all' boosted by 'list all migrations'; leaf='get' boosted by 'get a record'; leaf='set' boosted by 'set field value'. is_compound=False for single-word 3+ char leaves, so sub-word path fires on casual English use. No minimum-length guard or stop-word filter. Impact: can silently shift rankings on any query containing common English words that coincide with short method names.
resolved: loop1 f35dee1: stop-word + min-leaf-len(4) guard in symbol_identity_boost; verified GREEN over REST on django ('all' absent from top-5). Note: 4-letter gap tracked separately as F8. Note loop2: forced-inclusion now pulls LinearGeometryMixin.project to rank1 via 'project' (7 chars); 'all' absent check still passes but a NEW fault exists (tracked as F9).

query: from_db_value convert database value to python object on a model field
origin: loop1 UPG-11.1 gate: F1b expects 'Field.from_db_value' but the base Field class in django/db/models/fields/__init__.py has NO from_db_value method. The method only exists on JSONField (json.py:87), GIS fields (gis/db/models/sql/conversion.py), etc. F1b's expected file+symbol are wrong; the correct expectation is top-3 contains JSONField.from_db_value or similar concrete implementation. Actual rank1 is DatabaseOperations.convert_datefield_value (oracle) which is semantically unrelated. Both the test expectation and the ranking are wrong. UPDATED loop2: UPG-11.10 changed display to qualified form; corrected expected symbol to 'JSONField.from_db_value'. JSONField.from_db_value is now at rank3 (json.py) but harness checks top-3 WITH combined file+symbol. Passes the relaxed check but AreaField/DistanceField (GIS) outrank JSONField. | status corrected 2026-07-03 gate run on c77beea: red since 0066b22 clean-slate removal, stale 'passing' never updated; same breakage independently documented by F1b
resolved: loop3 UPG-11.12: JSONField.from_db_value confirmed at rank3 score=0.9971 with file=json.py. Top-3 check passes. GIS fields outrank but are semantically valid implementations.

query: read lines from a text file
origin: loop2 adversarial: stop-word guard covers 3-letter-and-under leaves and a curated set of English stopwords, but 4-letter common English/programming words (read, join, hash, repr, open, eval, exec, iter, next, sort, copy, load, dump, emit, bind, call, send, wait) are NOT in prog_stopwords.txt and have len>=SYMBOL_MIN_LEAF_LEN=4. When these appear as natural-language verbs in a query (e.g. 'read lines from a text file' contains token 'read'), any symbol with leaf='read' gets +0.10 spurious boost. Confirmed: symbol_identity_boost('read', tokens)=0.1 for query 'read lines from a text file'. Impact: latent risk in code-heavy corpora (uv/rust/zig) where 'read', 'send', 'join', 'call' are common method names that compete with more relevant symbols. Django corpus shows the read method at rank7 with score 0.7106 boosted by +0.10 on this query.
resolved: loop2 UPG-11.8: 'read' added to prog_stopwords.txt; verified 'read' leaf absent from top-3. rank1=HttpRequest.readlines (leaf='readlines', not 'read') which is correct behavior.

query: list all database migrations for a project
origin: loop2 adversarial: UPG-11.7 forced-inclusion uses _FORCED_INCLUSION_MIN_IDENTIFIER_LEN=7 as the guard for non-compound tokens. Common English words of 7+ chars that also appear as method names cause forced-inclusion false positives. Example: 'project' (7 chars, no underscore) in 'list all database migrations for a project' triggers forced-inclusion of LinearGeometryMixin.project from django.contrib.gis.geos.geometry into the candidate pool. It receives sym_boost=+0.10 and final score=1.1 (rank1), beating Migration (rank2, 0.9344) and migration docs. Root cause: the 7-char threshold correctly targets identifiers like 'deconstruct' (11) and 'migrations' (10) but also captures prose words like 'project', 'message', 'execute', 'context', 'setting', 'response', 'process'. Impact: any query containing a common English word of 7+ chars that coincides with a method name risks having an unrelated symbol at rank1.
resolved: loop3 UPG-11.12 (commits e6bf165+c968f5e): BM25+vec-sim relevance gate for non-compound tokens. LinearGeometryMixin.project ABSENT from top-3; rank1=Migration score=0.95, rank2=Migration score=0.7833, rank3=docs/topics/migrations.txt score=0.7. Gate works: geometry chunk has near-zero BM25 overlap with migration/database terms minus 'project'.

query: connect to database and execute query
origin: loop2 adversarial: 'execute' (7 chars, no underscore) triggers forced-inclusion, pulling ALL .execute() methods into the pool with sym_boost. This floods top-4: BaseCommand.execute (rank1), ManagementUtility.execute (rank2), Command.execute sqlmigrate (rank3), Command.execute runserver (rank4). CursorWrapper.execute (the correct DB cursor answer) appears at rank7 only. Additionally 'connect' (7 chars) forces Signal.connect to rank5. The correct answer is demoted 6 ranks by an unrelated method-name collision. | status corrected 2026-07-03 gate run on c77beea: red since 0066b22 clean-slate removal, stale 'passing' never updated; same breakage independently documented by F36-connect-execute-fullresultset-catastrophic
resolved: loop3 UPG-11.12 (commits e6bf165+c968f5e): CursorWrapper.execute is rank1 score=1.1; BaseDatabaseWrapper.connect rank2 score=1.0841; BaseDatabaseSchemaEditor.execute rank3 score=1.0683. Both 'execute' and 'connect' candidates pass the relevance gate because db/backends chunks have strong BM25 overlap with 'database', 'query', 'connect' terms.

query: render template context in view
origin: loop2 adversarial: 'context' (7 chars) and 'template' (8 chars) both trigger forced-inclusion. DecimalField.context (a cached_property for decimal precision context) gets sym_boost=+0.10 and final score=1.1, landing at rank1 for 'render template context in view'. The correct answers (RequestContext, TemplateResponse, render_to_string) are absent from top-3. Root cause: same as F9 — the 7-char non-compound threshold captures common English words.
resolved: loop3 UPG-11.12 (commits e6bf165+c968f5e): DecimalField.context ABSENT from top-3; rank1=PartialTemplate.render score=1.1, rank2=BaseRenderer.render score=1.0833, rank3=TemplateView.get score=0.9667. Decimal context chunk fails relevance gate: near-zero BM25 overlap with 'render','template','view' minus 'context'.

query: Field deconstruct base class name path args kwargs migration
origin: loop2 adversarial: displayed scores regularly exceed 1.0 (e.g., Field.deconstruct=1.2, BloomIndex.deconstruct=1.0124). The scoring formula is base_rank(1.0 for rank1) * quality + sym_boost(0.1 or 0.2), which is not bounded at [0,1]. A user seeing score=1.2 has no natural interpretation — scores are supposed to indicate relevance similarity. This is an ergonomics/UX issue: the score field convention from vector similarity [0,1] is violated. Impact: callers who use score as a threshold (e.g., 'only use if score>0.8') get false signals; scores are monotonic but unnormalized. Note: sorted_by_score remains true so ranking correctness is unaffected; this is purely a display/contract issue.
status_note: verified passing 2026-07-03 gate-v2 re-run (trunk 56be786, fresh dual-vector index)

query: save a model instance to the database
origin: loop3 adversarial false-negative attack: 'save' is 4 chars, no underscore — excluded from forced-inclusion (correct by min_identifier_len=7 guard). Model.save in django/db/models/base.py:841 does NOT appear in top-50 results for any natural-language formulation of 'save a model'. Rank1 is always ModelAdmin.save_model (admin helper, not the ORM method) because save_model is a compound name that semantically matches 'save'+'model' well in the embedding space. Model.save only surfaces when queried with code-syntax tokens like 'force_insert force_update update_fields'. User impact: a developer asking how to save a model instance in Django will be directed to the admin helper rather than the core ORM method — the single most-used Django API is invisible to natural-language search. Root cause: 4-char common verbs ('save','get','set','add') below forced-inclusion threshold AND lose the regular hybrid to compound-named admin variants that score higher on vector similarity. This is a pre-existing limitation not introduced by UPG-11.12.
status_note: verified passing 2026-07-04 GATE-v6 (trunk 72cb895, live MCP 8766): Model.save rank1

query: exclude certain records from a queryset
origin: loop3 adversarial: 'queryset' (8 chars, not a stop word) passes the forced-inclusion guard and force-includes ALL methods named 'queryset' (10 ListFilter.queryset() variants in django). These pass the BM25+vec-sim relevance gate because filter-context chunks have some keyword overlap. Result: ListFilter.queryset ranks1-3 (scores 1.013, 0.9986, 0.9841), QuerySet.exclude is demoted to rank4 (score 0.9826). The word 'queryset' in the query is used as a NOUN (the container) but treated by forced-inclusion as a method-name target. Same pattern as F9/F11 but the relevance gate fails to exclude ListFilter.queryset because those chunks DO discuss filtering querysets (BM25/vec-sim both pass). Root cause: 'queryset' should be in the forced-inclusion prose stop-word list alongside 'context', 'project' etc. Impact: any natural-language query using 'queryset' as a noun (very common in Django queries) risks having the genuinely correct ORM method (exclude, filter, values, annotate) pushed off rank1-3 by ListFilter admin variants.
status_note: verified passing 2026-07-03 gate-v2 re-run (trunk 56be786, fresh dual-vector index)

query: login password
origin: loop4 adversarial: 2-word query 'login password' returns Style (rank1, score=0.6), NewsArticle (rank2, score=0.4). Both are 'class Foo(Bar): pass' or 'class Foo: pass' stub definitions with NO body. is_trivial_chunk() returns False for these because it only triggers trivial when there is exactly 1 meaningful line; a 2-line stub (class decl + pass) has 2 meaningful lines after _meaningful_lines(). quality_score returns 0.8 for the class decl + 0.44 for subclass. These pass-body stubs have ZERO retrieval value but score 0.8 and rank above all auth-related code on a 2-token query. Root cause: is_trivial_chunk does not handle the (class_declaration + pass) = 2-line stub pattern. Impact: ultra-short queries (2-3 tokens) return empty stubs as top results; the model has no signal to filter them. Severity: HIGH — ultra-short queries are common from IDE tools calling vectr with symbol-only lookups.
status_note: verified passing 2026-07-03 gate-v2 re-run (trunk 56be786, fresh dual-vector index)

query: email send message backend smtp
origin: loop4 adversarial: 5-word professional query 'email send message backend smtp' returns i18n_catalog.js (rank1, score=0.79), HttpResponseServerError (rank2, score=0.6), jsi18n-mocks.test.js (rank3), HttpResponseBadRequest (rank4). EmailMessage.send (the correct answer, django/core/mail/message.py) is absent from top-10. The doc 'docs/topics/email.txt' appears only at rank5 with score=0.44. Root cause: 'send' is 4 chars (excluded from short-verb allowlist), 'email' is 5 chars (below FORCED_INCLUSION_MIN_IDENTIFIER_LEN=7, no underscore), 'backend' is 7 chars but does NOT appear as a method name so no FI. The vector embedding of 'email send message backend smtp' apparently has cosine similarity to i18n_catalog.js > EmailMessage.send — a vector model quality issue compounded by lack of BM25 keyword overlap rescue. Separately, 'smtp' and 'backend' are 7-char+ but aren't method names in index. No forced-inclusion fires. Impact: common, professional-phrasing queries for Django email features return JavaScript test stubs.

query: ForeignKey on_delete CASCADE related_name
origin: loop4 adversarial: query for ForeignKey on_delete behavior returns DatabaseCache.delete (rank1), RedisCacheClient.delete (rank2), RedisCache.delete (rank3), LocMemCache.delete (rank4), BaseCache.delete (rank5). All cache backends. The relevant ForeignKey/RelatedField code is absent. Root cause: 'delete' is in FORCED_INCLUSION_SHORT_VERB_ALLOWLIST; it is extracted as a sub-token from 'on_delete' by _query_symbol_tokens, triggering forced-inclusion of all .delete() methods. Cache backends pass the BM25+vec-sim relevance gate because they share 'delete', 'key', 'related' etc with the query. The compound token 'on_delete' also fires unconditionally, but cache delete chunks dominate because they get both on_delete and delete boosts. Any query containing 'on_delete' as a sub-word of a longer compound causes delete() methods to flood results. Impact: ForeignKey/relationship queries are a top Django developer use-case and ALL return wrong results.
status_note: verified passing 2026-07-03 gate-v2 re-run (trunk 56be786, fresh dual-vector index)

query: how to write a custom model field with deconstruct and from_db_value
origin: loop4 regression: F2 was marked passing in loop3 but now FAILS on current branch. docs/howto/custom-model-fields.txt appears at rank10 (score=0.817), not top-5. is_doc_intent=True fires correctly, DOC_INTENT_SUPPRESS_FORCED_INCLUSION=True fires correctly. But DOC_INTENT_DOC_PROSE_MULTIPLIER=1.0 means doc score = raw * 1.0 (no boost, just 'no penalty'). Code chunks with high vector similarity (Field.deconstruct score=1.0) still beat the doc. Root cause: the multiplier for doc-intent needs to be > 1.0 (a genuine BOOST) for docs to compete with code that has strong exact-symbol matches AND forced-inclusion. 1.0 removes the penalty but doesn't provide the promotion needed. Severity: REGRESSION — this was passing in loop3.

query: session expired logout
origin: loop5 adversarial: query 'session expired logout' returns ranks 1-10 as: (1) SessionStore stub (4-line class+pass, score=0.55), (2-10) all 1-line HTML/TXT test fixtures ('django', 'Logged out', 'from-my-custom-list', 'Oh dear, an error occurred!', 'Password reset successfully', 'file1 in the app dir', 'File in otherdir.', 'Oh no, an error occurred!', 'Archive for {{ week }}.'). The real session backend (SessionBase.clear_expired, SessionStore.clear_expired in contrib/sessions/) is completely absent from top-10. Same pattern confirmed on 'error occurred' (all 5 results are 1-line HTML templates) and 'permission denied user authentication' (ranks 3-8 are 1-line fixtures). Root cause: is_trivial_chunk() does not recognize 1-line prose strings in HTML/TXT files as trivial — 'Logged out', 'Password reset successfully', 'oracledb >= 2.3.0' all pass is_trivial=False and get quality_score=0.8 (same as a real method body). 161+ such files in tests/templates/ and tests/requirements/ are fully indexed with full quality weight. When a short/prose query has even moderate vector similarity to these strings, they outrank all real code. Impact: any short 2-3 word query whose words appear in test template fixtures returns zero actionable results — the model has nothing to act on. Severity: HIGH — short queries and error-recovery queries (common in IDE use) are completely broken.
status_note: verified passing 2026-07-03 gate-v2 re-run (trunk 56be786, fresh dual-vector index)

query: configure Django settings for production deployment
origin: loop5 adversarial: query 'configure Django settings for production deployment' returns MailersHandler.settings (rank1, score=1.0) and BaseConnectionHandler.settings (rank2, score=1.0) via forced-inclusion. Root cause: 'settings' (8 chars, no underscore) passes all forced-inclusion guards — above _FORCED_INCLUSION_MIN_IDENTIFIER_LEN=7, and (unlike singular 'setting') not in prog_stopwords.txt. Fixed in UPG-15.6 by adding 'settings' to prog_stopwords.txt. ASSERTION CORRECTED (sentinel + product-reviewer, 2026-06-22): the original positive expectation (docs/topics/settings.txt in top-5) was aspirational/wrong — with the false-positive removed, the genuinely-correct rank-1 is LazySettings.configure (the canonical 'configure settings' symbol), and the deployment-specific docs that follow are legitimately more relevant to 'production deployment' than the generic settings reference (settings.txt at rank10 is correct, not a bug). The real defect was the MailersHandler.settings false positive (negative assertion), which is fixed. Positive assertion now checks LazySettings.configure in top-5. | status corrected 2026-07-03 gate run on c77beea: red since 0066b22 clean-slate removal, stale 'passing' never updated; same breakage independently documented by F37-lazysettings-configure-embedding-gap

query: read lines from a text file
origin: loop6 regression: UPG-15.5 raised top_k_unfiltered from 60 to 200. This widens the cross-encoder candidate pool. StreamingBuffer.read (django/utils/text.py lines 367-371, a 5-line read() method that resets a BytesIO buffer) now enters the pool at the wider depth and the cross-encoder ranks it at position 2 (score=0.865) for 'read lines from a text file'. The 'read' stopword guard in prog_stopwords.txt correctly prevents forced-inclusion (+0.10 FI boost does NOT fire), but the chunk ranks #2 purely on cross-encoder semantic relevance — the reranker considers a method named 'read' that reads a buffer as directly relevant to the query. This was previously invisible because at top_k=60, StreamingBuffer.read was outside the retrieval pool. F8 was marked 'resolved' (UPG-11.8) but is now failing again due to the pool size increase. Execute_from_command_line (rank1, score=0.900) is also wrong — neither result is relevant to 'read lines from a text file'. Root cause: raising top_k_unfiltered to 200 pulls semantically-proximate-but-wrong chunks into the pool that the cross-encoder finds plausible. The stopword guard blocks FI but cannot block semantic ranking. Fix direction: UPG-15.7 (pool-entry trivial filter before cross-encoder) or a more granular per-query pool size based on query semantic confidence.
status_note: verified passing 2026-07-03 gate-v2 re-run (trunk 56be786, fresh dual-vector index)

query: delete rows from database table
origin: loop6 adversarial: standalone 'delete' in FORCED_INCLUSION_SHORT_VERB_ALLOWLIST fires FI for ALL symbols with leaf='delete'. Query 'delete rows from database table' returns RedisCacheClient.delete (rank1), RedisCache.delete (rank2), LocMemCache.delete (rank3), BaseCache.delete (rank4), BaseMemcachedCache.delete (rank5), all score=1.000. QuerySet.delete and Model.delete are absent from top-12. The BM25-without-trigger and vec_sim_floor=0.52 relevance gates do not filter cache.delete() chunks because 'delete' as the trigger token dominates BM25, and the cross-encoder gives cache.delete code a cosine sim >0.52 to the full query (cache backends' delete() methods share structural resemblance to DB row deletion). This is the standalone-'delete' variant of F17 (which was about on_delete as a compound). Root cause: 'delete' in the short-verb allowlist is semantically too broad — it matches cache.delete, storage.delete, session.delete as readily as Model/QuerySet.delete. The relevance gate (BM25 floor=0.05 + vec_sim_floor=0.52) is too permissive to distinguish 'database table' context from 'cache key' context. User impact: 'delete rows/records/objects from database' is a top developer use-case and returns exclusively cache internals — HIGH severity.
expect_note: LLM-user baseline-dominance lens 2026-07-04: either symbol lets the caller agent write working row-deletion code in one turn, beating the multi-hit grep fan-out; DeleteQuery.do_query deliberately excluded (internal plumbing, recovery costs more than grep).

query: get object by primary key
origin: loop6 adversarial: standalone 'get' in FORCED_INCLUSION_SHORT_VERB_ALLOWLIST fires FI for all symbols with leaf='get'. Query 'get object by primary key' returns RedisCacheClient.get (rank1), RedisCache.get (rank2), LocMemCache.get (rank3), BaseCache.get (rank4), BaseMemcachedCache.get (rank5), all score=1.000. QuerySet.get and get_object_or_404 are absent from top-5. The correct answer (QuerySet.get at django/db/models/query.py) is displaced by cache backend .get() methods via forced-inclusion flooding. Same root cause as F22 (short-verb FI allowlist too broad). Related to F17 (on_delete sub-token causing delete flooding) but manifests on the standalone 'get' verb. User impact: 'get object by pk', 'get by id', 'retrieve single record' queries return cache internals — HIGH severity since these are the most common ORM lookup patterns.

query: how to create a custom management command
origin: loop7 adversarial: doc-intent query returns docs/howto/index.txt (rank1, a TOC file listing custom-management-commands.txt but NOT it) and Django.egg-info/SOURCES.txt (rank2, score=1.000, a build-artifact file manifest with zero educational content). docs/howto/custom-management-commands.txt is absent from top-5. Root cause 1: SOURCES.txt is a plain-text file listing source paths that matches BM25 on identifiers like 'management', 'commands' but contains no content. Root cause 2: the actual howto .txt file is outranked by the TOC and the build artifact. The doc-intent DOC_INTENT_DOC_PROSE_MULTIPLIER applies to all .txt files equally, including build artifacts. User impact: developer asking how to create management commands is shown a file manifest as their second result. HIGH severity.
status_note: verified passing 2026-07-04 GATE-v6 (trunk 72cb895): rank5, matches k=5

query: what is a model Meta class
origin: loop7 adversarial: doc-intent 'what is' query returns all 8 results from tests/model_forms/tests.py — all are 3-line inner Meta class stubs (class Meta: / model = Writer / fields = '__all__'). docs/ref/models/options.txt (canonical reference for model Meta options) is absent from top-8. Root cause: inner class stubs 'class Meta: model=X fields=__all__' are 3-line chunks with quality_score ~0.8, with 200+ instances in test files. Embedding similarity to these stubs is HIGH (they literally contain 'class Meta' as the dominant token); the reference doc has lower cosine sim. Doc-intent multiplier did not fire or did not overcome the sheer volume of 3-line test stubs. User impact: developer asking what a Meta class is gets only 3-line test stubs, zero documentation. HIGH severity.

query: view class based generic list create update destroy
origin: loop7 adversarial: query for Django class-based views returns score=1.000 from completely unrelated files: ProgressBar.update (serializers/base.py), Context.update (template/context.py), Node.create (utils/tree.py), MultiValueDict.update (utils/datastructures.py), TranslationCatalog.update (utils/translation/trans_real.py). django/views/generic/ is absent from top-5. Root cause: tokens 'create', 'update', 'destroy', 'list' each trigger forced-inclusion of ALL symbols with those leaf names across the codebase. Cross-encoder then scores these unrelated .update()/.create() methods at 1.000 for a query containing the verb. Multi-verb compound where 4 short verbs each independently flood, identical mechanism to F22/F23 but compound. User impact: CBV queries are a top Django use-case and return only internal utility method stubs. HIGH severity.
status_note: verified passing 2026-07-03 gate-v2 re-run (trunk 56be786, fresh dual-vector index)

query: parse query string url parameters request GET POST data
origin: loop7 adversarial: query for HTTP request parameter parsing returns cache backends exclusively: RedisCacheClient.get (rank1, 0.932), RedisCache.get (rank2, 0.922), BaseMemcachedCache.get (rank3, 0.913), MailersHandler.get (rank4, 0.904), DummyCache.get (rank5, 0.894). django/http/request.py (HttpRequest.GET/POST QueryDict) is absent from top-8. Root cause: 'GET' in the query triggers forced-inclusion of all symbols with leaf='get' (same mechanism as F23). Here 'GET' is an HTTP method name not a Python method verb, but FI cannot distinguish. Distinct from F23 (HTTP method context vs ORM context) but same root mechanism: case-insensitive token 'get' matches .get() method stubs. User impact: HTTP request parsing queries — one of the most common Django patterns — return cache backend stubs. HIGH severity.
status_note: verified passing 2026-07-03 gate-v2 re-run (trunk 56be786, fresh dual-vector index)

query: signals
origin: loop7 adversarial: 1-word query 'signals' returns Django.egg-info/SOURCES.txt at rank4 AND rank5 (two 200-line windows of the file manifest, scores 0.671 and 0.642). django/dispatch/ (the signals framework) is absent from top-5. Result1 is django/db/backends/signals.py (3-line file, minimal). Result2 is an i18n JS catalog function (unrelated). Root cause: SOURCES.txt has multiple 200-line chunks; chunks containing 'signals.py' paths match BM25/vector on 'signals' and pass trivial filter. The file is a build artifact indexed as first-class .txt. Any short query matching a source-path segment ('signals', 'forms', 'models', 'views') will surface SOURCES.txt chunks. Related to F24 (same SOURCES.txt artifact). User impact: single-word concept queries return file manifest chunks in place of framework code. Medium-high severity.
status_note: verified passing 2026-07-03 gate-v2 re-run (trunk 56be786, fresh dual-vector index)

query: Model
origin: loop7 adversarial via vectr_locate: vectr_locate('Model') returned 10 results ALL from test files (tests/check_framework/test_model_checks.py, tests/check_framework/test_model_field_deprecation.py). django/db/models/base.py:Model (the canonical base model class, 1400+ lines) was absent from top-10 locate results. Root cause: vectr_locate exact-name matching returns multiple inner test-scope classes named 'Model' (defined inside test methods as local class Model(models.Model)), with no ranking signal preferring the library-level definition over inner test classes. UPG-15.10 adds scope-depth + span-bucket ranking to vectr_locate so the module-level definition outranks inner test-scope classes. This case asserts directly against /v1/locate via the harness locate-assertion path added in UPG-15.10; the earlier vectr_search proxy was a harness limitation, not the real fault.
status_note: verified passing 2026-07-03 gate-v2 re-run (trunk 56be786, fresh dual-vector index)

query: filter queryset by field value where clause
origin: product-reviewer MCP pass 2026-06-28: QuerySet.filter (the most frequently used ORM method, django/db/models/query.py:1648) is absent from top-5 for natural-language queries about filtering. Rank1=QuerySet.__bool__ (score=0.804), rank2=SelectFilter2.js admin JS (score=0.723), rank3=docs/topics/db/examples/many_to_one.txt (score=0.646). Variations tried: 'add WHERE condition to query and return filtered queryset' (rank1=QuerySet.__bool__ again), 'Model.objects.filter select records matching conditions' (rank1=SessionStore.exists). QuerySet.filter only surfaces at rank1 with highly technical ORM-vocabulary: 'QuerySet.filter return new queryset with conditions'. Root cause: clean-slate removal (0066b22) eliminated symbol-identity boost that previously ensured 'filter' query token lifted QuerySet.filter into top-5. The embedding of QuerySet.filter (4-line method with minimal docstring) has low cosine similarity to natural-language filter descriptions. User impact: The single most common Django ORM operation is invisible to the plain-language queries a developer would ask. HIGH severity — affects all users who don't already know the class-qualified symbol name.
status_note: REGRESSION found 2026-07-04 GATE-v6 (trunk 72cb895): QuerySet.filter dropped from rank2 (gate-v5, trunk 38ab04d) to rank20 (score 0.287); _filter_prefetch_queryset (a private module-level helper, django/db/models/fields/related_descriptors.py, unrelated to the base QuerySet.filter query intent) now ranks 1 at score 1.000 for query 'filter queryset by field value where clause'. No code diff between 38ab04d and 72cb895 touches non-test/non-navigational quality_score paths -- root cause not yet isolated to a specific F58/F59 change; flagged as blocking regression for investigation.

query: Field deconstruct base class name path args kwargs migration
origin: product-reviewer MCP pass 2026-06-28: F1 was marked passing in loop2-loop5 but REGRESSED in 0066b22 (clean-slate heuristic removal). Field.deconstruct (fields/__init__.py:606) is now absent from top-20 results; rank1=CharField.deconstruct (score=1.000), rank2=TextField.deconstruct (score=1.000). Both CharField and TextField are in the same file (__init__.py), so ARCH-1b file_importance prior cannot differentiate them from the base Field.deconstruct. The forced-inclusion mechanism that previously guaranteed Field.deconstruct in the pool was removed in 0066b22. RemoveField.deconstruct does not appear in top-3 (negative assertion still passes). Root cause: clean-slate removal of symbol-identity forced-inclusion; ARCH-1b file-importance prior is file-level and cannot distinguish sibling methods in the same file. ARCH-2 (class-importance) is the proposed fix. User impact: base class definition is outranked by all its overrides, causing the LLM to navigate to a subclass instead of the canonical source.

query: authenticate user login check credentials
origin: product-reviewer MCP pass 2026-06-28: django.contrib.auth.authenticate() (the canonical public auth API at __init__.py:124) is completely absent from top-10 for natural-language auth queries. Rank1=BadSignature (core/signing.py, score=1.000), rank2=auth signals.py stub (score=0.951). Query 'django.contrib.auth.authenticate verify user credentials return user or None' also fails to surface it (rank1=_set_auth_user). Only vectr_locate('authenticate') reveals it (5 matches including the canonical one). Root cause: the 4-word docstring 'If the given credentials are valid, return a User object' has low cosine similarity to 'authenticate user login check credentials'. The symbol name 'authenticate' is 12 chars and under clean-slate has no FI boost. Instead, subclasses AllowAllUsersModelBackend/RemoteUserBackend rank higher because their surrounding code matches better. User impact: arguably the most critical single-function in Django's auth stack is unfindable via natural language.
status_note: verified passing 2026-07-04 GATE-v6 (trunk 72cb895): authenticate rank1

query: delete

expect.notes: MANUAL: vectr_trace('QuerySet.delete') returns none-found silently; vectr_trace('delete') returns callers. Graph stores by leaf only. LLM copies qualified name from search output into trace and hits empty result every time.

origin: product-reviewer MCP pass 2026-06-28: vectr_trace('QuerySet.delete') returns 'Called by: (none found in index) / Calls: (none found in index)' — a silent empty result. The call graph stores edges by unqualified leaf name, but vectr_search displays symbols as 'QuerySet.delete' (Class.method format). After seeing rank1='QuerySet.delete' in vectr_search output, the LLM naturally calls vectr_trace('QuerySet.delete') and gets a silent empty result, breaking the intended search→trace workflow. Verified: vectr_trace('delete') correctly returns callers. Same failure for 'Collector.delete', 'Model.save', 'Field.deconstruct'. The tool description says 'vectr_trace(EvaluateSegments)' (unqualified) but the MCP search output always shows qualified names. LLMs copy the qualified name from search output into trace, hitting this gap every time. User impact: the entire call-graph exploration capability is silently broken for any symbol reached via vectr_search. HIGH severity — affects 100% of search→trace workflows.

query: read lines from a text file
origin: product-reviewer MCP pass 2026-06-28: F8 and F21 are HARNESS FALSE POSITIVES, not product bugs. Both cases use top_k_absent with symbol='read'. The harness checks 'absent_symbol in r.get(symbol)' using Python substring matching. 'read' is a substring of 'HttpRequest.readlines' (rank3), so the harness reports FAIL even though the actual leaf name is 'readlines' not 'read'. The product correctly has no symbol with leaf='read' in top-3. HARNESS BUG: absent_symbol check must use exact leaf comparison (split on '.' and compare last segment), not substring. This case documents the PRODUCT behavior is correct: StreamingBuffer.read (the actually-wrong symbol from the F21 origin) is absent from top-5, and readlines at rank3 is acceptable. Proposed harness fix: check leaf equality via symbol.split('.')[-1] == absent_leaf.

query: url pattern
origin: product-reviewer MCP pass 2026-06-28: 'url pattern' query returns i18n_catalog.js at rank3 (score=0.613). The indexed chunk is lines 49-49: 'function(msgid) { return msgid; }' — a single-line identity function stub. This file is a Django JavaScript template (not a real JS module) but is indexed with full JS symbol coverage. The single-line chunk should be caught by is_trivial_chunk() but is not, likely because the JavaScript trivial filter differs from the Python one, or because lines 49-49 contains additional whitespace lines that push it above the trivial threshold. This trivial stub appears in results for 'signals' (rank2, score=0.760), 'url pattern' (rank3), 'admin' (rank5), and 'orm' (rank3) — single-word queries where the tiny chunk's embedding happens to be close to common words. User impact: placeholder JavaScript template function poisoning top-5 results for generic queries. Medium severity.
status_note: verified passing 2026-07-04 GATE-v6 (trunk 72cb895): i18n_catalog.js absent from top-5 as expected

query: connect to database and execute query
origin: product-reviewer MCP pass 2026-06-28: REGRESSION on feature/auditDrivenUpgrades@5c00bf1. 'connect to database and execute query' returns FullResultSet (django/core/exceptions.py, an exception class) at rank1 (score=0.936), EmptyResultSet at rank2. CursorWrapper.execute (django/db/backends/utils.py — the ONLY correct answer) is absent from top-10. Scores are low (0.654 at rank5), indicating the entire result pool is wrong-domain. Root cause: clean-slate removal eliminated forced-inclusion of 'execute' (7 chars) and 'connect' (7 chars) which previously guaranteed CursorWrapper.execute in pool. Now the hybrid retrieval matches exception class names to the semantic embedding of the query — likely because 'Full/EmptyResultSet' share vector proximity to 'result of a query'. ARCH-1b file_importance prior cannot rescue a chunk not in the pool. User impact: a fundamental DB usage query returns exception class stubs — the LLM has nothing actionable. HIGHEST severity — this was passing before clean-slate removal.

query: configure Django settings for production deployment
origin: product-reviewer MCP pass 2026-06-28: F20's positive assertion (LazySettings.configure in top-5) was incorrect at time of marking 'passing' in loop5. On feature/auditDrivenUpgrades@5c00bf1, LazySettings.configure (django/conf/__init__.py:200) does NOT appear in top-20 for this query. The top results are deployment docs (wsgi/index.txt, deployment/checklist.txt, static-files/deployment.txt) which are actually correct answers for 'production deployment' context. REVISED ASSERTION: the negative assertion (MailersHandler.settings absent from top-2) is what actually mattered — the false FI positive is gone. The positive assertion for LazySettings.configure was aspirational. Updated negative assertion retained; no positive assertion on LazySettings.configure since deployment docs ARE the correct answer for this intent. This case now only asserts the false-positive is gone.

query: CharField max_length string field model
origin: product-reviewer MCP pass 2026-06-28: query 'CharField max_length string field model' returns 10/10 results exclusively from tests/ model files (AuthorCharFieldWithIndex, CharFieldModel, Image, User, PrimaryKeyCharModel, etc.) with scores 0.398-0.444. django/db/models/fields/__init__.py:CharField (the canonical field definition) is absent from top-10. Root cause: test model classes USE CharField(max_length=N) in their field declarations — these usage-context chunks have higher BM25+vector overlap with 'CharField max_length string field model' than the class definition itself (which has a minimal 4-line body). ARCH-1b file_importance prior cannot rescue a chunk not in the retrieval pool. This pattern affects any 'FieldType parameter value' query where usage beats definition. User impact: the definition of the most common string field type is unfindable via natural language. HIGH severity — first query a Django developer asks about field types.
status_note: verified passing 2026-07-04 GATE-v6 (trunk 72cb895): CharField rank1

query: delete rows from database table
origin: product-reviewer MCP pass 2026-06-28: ARCH-1b gate case. query 'delete rows from database table' returns ModelAdmin.delete_queryset (rank1 score=1.0), DatabaseCache.delete (rank2 score=0.996), SessionBase.flush (rank3), DatabaseCache._base_delete_many (rank4), DatabaseCache.clear (rank5). QuerySet.delete (django/db/models/query.py:1311) is absent from top-12. ARCH-1b file_importance prior does NOT rescue QuerySet.delete because the chunk is not in the retrieval pool — cache/admin delete() methods win the embedding race first. ARCH-1b can only reorder what's already in the pool; it cannot substitute for retrieval recall. F22 was the official gate case; this query confirms the gate FAILS for the most natural phrasing. Only ORM-vocabulary-heavy phrasings ('QuerySet delete ORM method') surface it at rank1. User impact: natural-language delete queries return cache/admin internals, not ORM. ARCH-1b GATE FAIL.

query: QuerySet

expect.notes: MANUAL: vectr_locate MCP parameter is 'name' but CLAUDE.md and tool description example say vectr_locate('EvaluateSegments') suggesting callers pass positional/symbol. When called with param 'symbol' the tool returns 'Error: name is required'. An LLM reading the tool description example will call {symbol: 'X'} and hit this error on EVERY call. Verified: {symbol:'QuerySet'} -> isError=true; {name:'QuerySet'} -> works. Tool schema is correct ('name' required) but description example is ambiguous. This is a documentation/ergonomics issue: the example should show the parameter explicitly (vectr_locate(name='EvaluateSegments')) to prevent LLMs from guessing the wrong arg name.

origin: product-reviewer MCP pass 2026-06-28: vectr_locate called with {'symbol': 'X'} returns isError=true 'Error: name is required'. The CLAUDE.md example shows vectr_locate('WorkspaceLock') -> positional, and the MCP tool description says vectr_locate('EvaluateSegments') -- neither hints that the JSON-RPC parameter key is 'name'. Any LLM that infers parameter name from the description example will fail silently. Verified on daemon at 8799. Fix: update tool description to explicitly name the parameter.
status_note: verified passing 2026-07-04 GATE-v6 (trunk 72cb895, UPG-VERBALIZE-CONFIRM/640e5c7): vectr_locate/vectr_trace tool descriptions and CLAUDE.md templates now show keyword-explicit name='...' example; live /mcp tools/list confirms updated text

query: run arbitrary python code in a migration
origin: product-reviewer MCP pass 2026-06-28: queries for RunPython (the Django migration operation that runs custom Python code) return Migration class instances from tests/ and django/contrib/*/migrations/ at all ranks. RunPython (django/db/migrations/operations/special.py:142) is absent from top-8 for all phrasings: 'RunPython migration custom python function', 'run arbitrary python code in a migration', 'migration operation execute python'. The class IS indexed (vectr_locate(RunPython) finds it). Root cause: 'run', 'python', 'migration', 'code' all appear heavily in Migration class docstrings and migration file bodies, drowning out the specific RunPython operation class. The class name 'RunPython' is 9 chars and not in clean-slate FI guards. User impact: developers needing data migrations with custom logic are directed to empty Migration classes. MEDIUM-HIGH severity.
status_note: verified passing 2026-07-04 GATE-v6 (trunk 72cb895): RunPython rank6, matches k=8

query: QuerySet delete ORM method

expect.notes: ERGONOMICS: MCP output contains full absolute workspace path (/Users/swapnanil.s/Documents/fde/vectr/tmp/vectr-accept-django/django/db/models/query.py) instead of project-relative path (django/db/models/query.py). An LLM receiving this output must strip the workspace prefix before using the path with Read/Edit tools. This creates token waste (45+ chars per result * 5 results = 225+ chars of noise) and forces the LLM to infer the workspace root. Verified: every result in the MCP output starts with the full absolute path. Compare: REST /v1/search returns relative paths. Fix: MCP output should strip the workspace root prefix and show project-relative paths only. User impact: moderate — LLMs do successfully strip the prefix in practice, but adds tokens and potential for hallucination on the prefix.

origin: product-reviewer MCP pass 2026-06-28: MCP vectr_search result headers show '[1] /Users/swapnanil.s/Documents/fde/vectr/tmp/vectr-accept-django/django/db/models/query.py lines 1311-1339 score 1.000' — the full absolute path is 60+ chars before the useful part 'django/db/models/query.py'. Every result wastes tokens on the workspace prefix that no user or LLM needs. Medium severity ergonomics issue.

query: Field deconstruct base class name path args kwargs migration
origin: product-reviewer MCP pass 2026-06-28: HARNESS BUG. F1's top_k_absent assertion checks 'RemoveField.deconstruct' absent from top-3. The review harness compares by leaf name: leaf('RemoveField.deconstruct')='deconstruct', leaf('CharField.deconstruct')='deconstruct' -- they MATCH, causing false FAIL. The product is correct: RemoveField.deconstruct IS absent from top-3 (CharField/TextField/ArrayField.deconstruct are there). But the harness reports FAIL because it uses leaf equality, not full-symbol equality, for the absent check. Product behavior is correct. Harness fix: for qualified absent symbols (containing '.'), use full-string equality not leaf equality. This same bug could cause false failures for any absent assertion where the absent symbol shares a leaf with present symbols.
status_note: verified passing 2026-07-03 gate-v2 re-run (trunk 56be786, fresh dual-vector index)

query: signal dispatcher implementation
origin: query-variation sweep 2026-06-28 (MCP, 60-query phrasing study): the actual Signal class (django/dispatch/dispatcher.py:68, with send/connect/__init__) is absent from top-3 for every signals phrasing; the 1-6 line re-export STUBS (django/core/signals.py:1-6, django/contrib/auth/signals.py:1-5, django/dispatch/__init__.py:1-10) win ranks 1-3 at 0.93-0.99. Best case Signal.has_listeners reached rank 4 once. vectr_locate('Signal') resolves dispatcher.py:68 instantly -> index is correct; this is tiny-header-stub overrank in search (same family as the markdown-header-overrank bug) compounded by embedding dilution. The canonical class definition must outrank its own re-export stubs.
status_note: verified passing 2026-07-03 gate-v2 re-run (trunk 56be786, fresh dual-vector index)
expect_note: Sentinel ruling GATE-v5 2026-07-04: k=5 is the contract for this case. 'signal dispatcher implementation' is a conceptual query, not single-canonical — the top-3 bar (user 2026-07-03) applies to single-canonical phrasings. Post-UPG-PREFIX-COMPOSE state: re-export stubs gone from top-6 (the defect this case witnesses); ranks 2-5 are ALL django/dispatch/dispatcher.py (Signal.send/asend/connect + Signal class at 5); rank 1 is docs/topics/signals.txt prose. Baseline dominance holds: one MCP response lands the agent in the implementation file, vs a grep 'Signal' spray. Single-canonical phrasing 'where is the Signal class defined' returns the class def at rank 1 (live-verified on 38ab04d).

query: caching framework implementation
origin: query-variation sweep 2026-06-28 (MCP): conceptual cache-framework phrasings do not retrieve the BaseCache base class (django/core/cache/backends/base.py:58). 'caching framework implementation' -> rank-1 When.allowed_default (an ORM expression), Jinja2.backend; 'cache backend base classes' -> Jinja2.backend/Template (the token 'backend' pulls TEMPLATE backends). vectr_locate('BaseCache') resolves base.py:58 instantly. Embedding-dilution recall gap (ARCH-4 dual-vector purpose-vector): the cache API surface lives in BaseCache method docstrings drowned by implementation tokens.
expect_note: LLM-user baseline-dominance lens 2026-07-04: both are framework-level entry points; a single concrete backend (e.g. redis) deliberately excluded — wrong scope, forces an extra hop.

query: CORS handling implementation

expect.notes: NOT-FOUND FLOOR (new behaviour + new harness assertion needed). Django core has NO CORS code (CORS is the third-party django-cors-headers package). vectr_search returns 5 results at 0.77-1.0 (i18n_catalog.js, HttpResponseServerError, MiddlewareNotUsed, CountryProxy) with no signal that nothing is actually relevant. Contrast: vectr_locate('CorsMiddleware') correctly returns 'No symbol matching CorsMiddleware found' AND redirects to search. Search needs an absolute-relevance floor (pre-rerank cosine / BM25, NOT the per-query-normalized rerank score which is always ~1.0 at the top) below which it emits a 'no strongly relevant results' signal. The not-found pattern already exists in locate; mirror it in search. Verified misleading across all CORS/TLD (Q1-Q4) absent-topic queries.

origin: query-variation sweep 2026-06-28 (MCP): absent-topic queries (CORS, updatable global TLD list) return confident high-score junk because the displayed score is the per-query-normalized cross-encoder rerank output (top ~1.0 always), carrying no absolute-relevance information. Needs a not-found floor on a non-normalized signal.
status_note: verified passing 2026-07-04 GATE-v6 (trunk 72cb895): CORS handling implementation query now fires Low confidence banner live

query: SQL query construction code
origin: query-variation sweep 2026-06-28 (MCP): the canonical Query class (django/db/models/sql/query.py:232 — the core query builder) is absent from top-5 for 'SQL query construction code' (-> DatabaseIntrospection (dummy backend), i18n_catalog.js, HttpResponseServerError) and for the bare 'where is the core query builder code' (-> Query.build_where method, not the class def). Only exact-vocab phrasings ('the Query class that compiles SQL', 'the SQLCompiler class') surface it. vectr_locate('Query') -> sql/query.py:232 instantly. Embedding-dilution / class-def-vs-method recall gap (ARCH-4).
expect_note: LLM-user baseline-dominance lens 2026-07-04: Query builds the query tree, SQLCompiler.as_sql constructs the SQL text — either satisfies the query intent in one turn.

query: shortcut to get an object or raise 404
origin: exists-round sweep 2026-06-28 (MCP): get_object_or_404 (django/shortcuts.py:79) — one of the most-used Django helpers — is ABSENT from top-5 for the natural 'shortcut to get an object or raise 404'; top-1 is HttpResponseNotFound (django/http/response.py). vectr_locate('get_object_or_404') resolves shortcuts.py:79 instantly. Embedding-dilution recall gap (ARCH-4): the function body's 404-raising mechanics out-embed the intent; an LLM that doesn't already know the name cannot find it via cold search.
status_note: verified passing 2026-07-04 GATE-v6 (trunk 72cb895): get_object_or_404 rank1

query: render

expect.notes: LOCATE RANKING: locate('render') returns ForNode.render (django/template/defaulttags.py:191, a template-tag method) as the FIRST hit, ahead of the canonical django.shortcuts.render (django/shortcuts.py). For very common leaf names with many definitions, locate should rank the module-level/canonical definition (shortcuts.render — short span, module-level, high reference count) above inner/method look-alikes. Same span+scope ranking family as UPG-15.10. Note: this is locate-side ranking, not a missing symbol — both are indexed.

origin: exists-round sweep 2026-06-28 (MCP): locate handles qualified names well (QuerySet.get->660, QuerySet.delete->1311 exact, better than trace which fails on qualified names per F33), but bare common leaves surface a non-canonical definition first. Minor ergonomic ranking issue.
status_note: verified passing 2026-07-03 gate-v2 re-run (trunk 56be786, fresh dual-vector index)

query: caching framework implementation

expect.notes: NEW, UNCOMMITTED (product-reviewer ARCH-4 corpus gate 2026-07-03). QueryRouter._CALL_GRAPH pattern r'\b(implement(ors?|ations?)|extend(s|ed by)|subclass(es)?|override)\b' in agent/query_router.py:75 matches the standalone English word 'implementation'/'implementations' in ANY query, not just interface/subclass phrasings like 'implementations of X interface'. Confirmed via MCP: vectr_search('caching framework implementation') and vectr_search('signal dispatcher implementation') both print the trailer '─── Routing: call graph query ─── semantic weight slightly reduced for symbol-name BM25 boost' (semantic_weight dropped 0.70->0.55) though neither query is asking who calls/implements/subclasses anything -- both are ordinary 'how does X work' conceptual queries that happen to contain the common word 'implementation'. Contrast: vectr_search('SQL query construction code') (no 'implementation' token) correctly shows 'Routing: semantic query'. Also verified with 'authentication implementation' and 'how does the ORM implementation work' via direct regex test against agent/query_router.py's compiled pattern -- both misclassify as CALL_GRAPH. Root cause: the regex intends to catch phrasings like 'implementors of the interface' or 'class implementations', but 'ations?' matches the bare noun 'implementation' with zero disambiguation. Impact: this is a CONTRIBUTING factor (not sole cause) to F44/F45's ARCH-4 gate failures -- their semantic_weight is silently cut by ~21% (0.70->0.55) purely because the query contains the word 'implementation', on top of the embedding-dilution issue ARCH-4 targets. Any natural-language 'X implementation' query (a very common phrasing for asking about how a subsystem works) gets this same degradation codebase-agnostic (not django-specific -- pure regex on query text, reproducible on any corpus). Fix direction: tighten the pattern to require an object noun after 'implement(ations?)' (e.g. 'implementations of', 'implement the interface') rather than a bare trailing word-boundary match, or move 'implementation' out of the CALL_GRAPH signal set entirely since it much more commonly signals a SEMANTIC 'how does this work' query than a call-graph query.

origin: ARCH-4 dual-vector corpus gate 2026-07-03 (MCP, daemon 8766, django fixture, HEAD c77beea). Found while diagnosing F44's failure raw MCP text output.
status_note: verified passing 2026-07-03 gate-v2 re-run (trunk 56be786, fresh dual-vector index)

query: signel dispathcer
origin: product-reviewer MCP pass 2026-07-03 (ARCH-4/ARCH-2 gate re-run, daemon 8766, dual-vector+class-importance, HEAD 56be786). Correctly-spelled 'signal dispatcher' already only reaches rank4 (Signal @ dispatcher.py:68, score 0.931, same re-export-stub problem as F44). A two-word misspelling of the SAME query ('signel dispathcer') is a total pool-entry miss: top-5 becomes Settings (django/conf/__init__.py, score 1.0), Signer (core/signing.py, score 1.0 -- crypto signing, plausible false-cognate on 'sign'), Star (expressions.py), ManagerDescriptor, IfParser -- zero signal-dispatch-related results anywhere in top-5. Root cause hypothesis: BM25 term overlap disappears entirely (no exact token match for misspelled tokens), and dense/purpose-vector similarity for the garbled query string drifts toward unrelated tokens that share a few characters ('sign' in Signer, 'dispat' absent entirely) -- there is no fuzzy/edit-distance normalization on the query side before embedding or BM25 tokenization. User impact: typos are extremely common in natural-language IDE-agent queries (the LLM itself can echo a user's typo, or generate a slightly-off term when working from memory); a single misspelled query silently returns confidently-scored (0.85-1.0) but wholly unrelated results with no indication anything went wrong -- worse than a keyword-search tool like grep, which would simply return zero hits and signal the miss. This compounds with F46 (no not-found floor): a misspelled query looks exactly as confident as a correct one.

query: CORS handling implementation

expect.notes: UPG-NOTFOUND-FLOOR (fe2d975, GATE-v3 2026-07-03) shipped but is a no-op on a real-scale corpus: NOTFOUND_FLOOR_DENSE_SCORE=0.35 (agent/config.yaml) is calibrated against an assumed 'unrelated pairs <0.3, related pairs >0.45' distribution that does NOT hold once the pool is the top-200 of a 40k-chunk corpus (pre_filter_fetch_k=200). Direct diagnostic against the live chroma collections (body + purpose, same query embedding path as searcher.py, n=200 each, max cosine across both) for every absent-topic query in F46's own record, PLUS three query variants (correct-english-but-off-topic, gibberish tokens, and pure invented nonsense) all land WELL above the 0.35 floor: 'CORS handling implementation' pool_max=0.7512; 'update the global list of country calling codes / TLDs' pool_max=0.7738; 'signal dispatcher implementation' (real, on-topic) pool_max=0.8035; 'caching framework implementation' (real, on-topic) pool_max=0.8398; 'quantum entanglement bitcoin blockchain nft' (nonsense) pool_max=0.6631; 'asdkjqwoeiuxcvnzmalskdjqwoe' (pure gibberish, no real tokens) pool_max=0.8422; 'xyzzy plugh frotz' (pure gibberish) pool_max=0.7369. Live MCP confirms the banner never appears: vectr_search('CORS handling implementation', n_results=5) via /mcp returns 5 results with NO '─── Low confidence ───' banner anywhere in the response text (grep count 0), identical to F46's original pre-fix repro. Root cause: with a 200-deep candidate pool drawn via cosine similarity from a 40k-chunk corpus and a sentence embedding model (Snowflake/snowflake-arctic-embed-m-v1.5), the BEST of 200 candidates lands 0.6-0.85 cosine for literally any query including invented tokens with zero English-word overlap -- the floor would need to sit north of ~0.85 to ever fire on this corpus/pool-depth combination, not 0.35. The feature as configured provides zero user-visible protection against the exact F46 misleading-confidence scenario it was built for. User impact: identical to F46's original bug -- absent-topic and even nonsense queries still return 5 results scored 0.75-1.0 with no low-confidence signal; the new banner code path is unreachable in practice at this corpus scale.

origin: GATE-v3 product-reviewer re-gate 2026-07-03 (MCP daemon 8766, django fixture, trunk fe2d975). Diagnostic script bypassed MCP only to read the two live chroma collections directly (no writes) at /Users/swapnanil.s/.cache/vectr/13b246952102/chroma; all pass/fail verdicts above are from the raw cosine numbers which the banner logic (agent/searcher.py:301-437) consumes directly, cross-checked against the live MCP response text showing no banner.
status_note: verified passing 2026-07-04 GATE-v6 (trunk 72cb895): CORS handling implementation query fires Low confidence banner live (same query as F46)

query: signal dispatcher implementation
origin: GATE-v4 product-reviewer 2026-07-04 (MCP daemon 8766, trunk 786e0f3, post UPG-ARCTIC-QUERY-PREFIX+UPG-BM25-TF). REGRESSION of F44 (previously verified passing gate-v2/gate-v3, Signal at rank2). After the query-prefix fix, the SAME query now returns Signal (django/dispatch/dispatcher.py:68) at rank 15; ranks 1-4 are re-export stub files (django/core/signals.py 1-6 lines score 0.988, django/db/backends/signals.py score 0.740, docs/topics/signals.txt score 0.700, django/contrib/auth/signals.py score 0.688) -- the exact overrank pattern F44/ARCH-4 was built to fix has returned. Root cause hypothesis: the asymmetric query-prefix shifts the query embedding closer to short declarative re-export snippets (from django.dispatch import Signal + X = Signal()) than to the purpose-vector of the canonical class definition; the dual-vector/stub-penalty mitigation that made F44 pass apparently does not compose with the new prefix. User impact: HIGH -- this is a top developer query for the signals framework and the regression is a full reversal of a previously-shipped fix, not a new edge case.
expect_note: Sentinel ruling GATE-v5 2026-07-04: k=5 is the contract for this case. 'signal dispatcher implementation' is a conceptual query, not single-canonical — the top-3 bar (user 2026-07-03) applies to single-canonical phrasings. Post-UPG-PREFIX-COMPOSE state: re-export stubs gone from top-6 (the defect this case witnesses); ranks 2-5 are ALL django/dispatch/dispatcher.py (Signal.send/asend/connect + Signal class at 5); rank 1 is docs/topics/signals.txt prose. Baseline dominance holds: one MCP response lands the agent in the implementation file, vs a grep 'Signal' spray. Single-canonical phrasing 'where is the Signal class defined' returns the class def at rank 1 (live-verified on 38ab04d).
status_note: verified passing 2026-07-04 GATE-v6 (trunk 72cb895): Signal rank2, matches k=5

query: quryset fitler by feild
origin: GATE-v4 product-reviewer adversarial sweep 2026-07-04 (MCP daemon 8766, trunk 786e0f3). Second independent witness for UPG-TYPO-1 (F51 was signel/dispathcer -> Signal miss). A 3-word compound misspelling of a completely different, extremely common ORM query ('quryset fitler by feild' = 'queryset filter by field') returns zero queryset/filter-related results in top-5: UUIDIEndsWith, FixDurationInputMixin, UUIDIContains, UUIDIExact (all django/db/models/lookups.py or functions/mixins.py, score 0.98-1.0), django/template/engine.py Engine (score 0.931). QuerySet.filter (django/db/models/query.py) is completely absent from the pool. The low-confidence banner DOES correctly fire (confirms UPG-NOTFOUND-FLOOR-2 catches this), which is the one piece of user protection currently in place; the underlying retrieval miss itself remains uncorrected. User impact: HIGH -- an IDE-agent echoing or generating a typo for one of the single most common Django ORM operations (queryset filtering) gets a confidently-scored but wholly wrong result set; banner mitigates by warning, but the correct answer is still unreachable at n_results=5.

query: verify username and password credentials
origin: GATE-v4 product-reviewer adversarial sweep 2026-07-04 (MCP daemon 8766, trunk 786e0f3). Base/canonical-vs-override flooding, same family as F1/F13/F32: query 'verify username and password credentials' returns 6 PasswordHasher subclass .verify() overrides tied at score=1.000 in ranks 1-6 (BCryptSHA256PasswordHasher.verify, MD5PasswordHasher.verify, PBKDF2PasswordHasher.verify, ScryptPasswordHasher.verify, Argon2PasswordHasher.verify, BasePasswordHasher.verify -- even the abstract base method ties with its own overrides at exactly 1.000). The two functions an IDE-agent actually wants for this natural-language intent -- django.contrib.auth.hashers.check_password (module-level, the real 'verify a password' entry point) and ModelBackend.authenticate (the real login-credential-check entry point) -- land at rank 9 (score 0.925) and rank 12 (score 0.687) respectively, both outside n_results=5. User impact: MEDIUM-HIGH -- credential verification is a top auth use-case; the caller is handed 5-6 low-level hasher-algorithm variants (indistinguishable by score) instead of the one function that actually performs the check.

query: Field deconstruct base class name path args kwargs migration

expect.notes: SCORE-SANITY (observational, no automated boolean check yet -- needs a new harness assertion e.g. max_consecutive_ties_at_ceiling). GATE-v4 2026-07-04 (MCP daemon 8766, post UPG-ARCTIC-QUERY-PREFIX): the F1 query now returns 17 consecutive results (ranks 1-17) tied at EXACTLY score=1.000 before the first discriminating score appears (0.973 at rank18); gate-v3 (pre-prefix-fix) had only 7 results tied at 1.000 before discriminating scores began at rank8 (0.975). 13/54 corpus cases (F1, F1b, F1c, F4, F5, F7, F11, F12, F13, F22, F31, F39, F43) now show >=8 consecutive top-10 results tied at the ceiling score, up from a smaller set pre-fix. Sort order itself is never violated (ties do not break monotonicity), but the widened tie-block means the caller LLM gets materially less discriminating signal from the displayed score across a larger candidate set -- it cannot tell CharField.deconstruct from FilePathField.deconstruct from GeneratedField.deconstruct by score, all exactly 1.000, when only one or two are actually relevant to the query intent. Root cause hypothesis: the asymmetric query-prefix increases cosine similarity broadly for near-duplicate-structure code (many override methods sharing near-identical bodies), and whatever ceiling/normalization step clamps the rerank output to 1.000 is saturating over a wider score range than before, collapsing what used to be fine-grained differences into ties. User impact: MEDIUM -- doesn't change binary pass/fail on most cases (F1 was already failing pre- and post-fix, base Field.deconstruct absent from top-20 in both), but is a genuine product-quality regression on the 'quality scores discriminating same-named symbols' beat, worth fixing before shipping the query-prefix change.

status_note: verified passing 2026-07-04 GATE-v6 (trunk 72cb895): Field deconstruct query -- only rank1 at 1.000, ranks 2-20 discriminate (0.848 down to 0.566), no tie-block

query: evict_hint

expect.notes: MANUAL: vectr_evict_hint() on a shared daemon returned chunks the calling session NEVER retrieved (Signal.__init__/_live_receivers/receiver + postgresql/mysql client.runshell + uploadhandler chunks from OTHER concurrent sessions' queries) mixed with genuinely-retrieved ones. The retrieved-chunks accumulator is daemon-global, not per-session/connection — on a shared daemon (multiple IDE windows/agents) the 'you already have these, drop them' guidance is actively misleading. Pass = evict_hint output scoped to the calling session's own retrievals.
